channel.title: Energy & Environment
channel.description: News, Analysis and Opinion from POLITICO

item.title: Trump's gas tax holiday pitch faces some early Capitol Hill headwinds
item.description: President Donald Trump’s push for a temporary suspension of the gasoline tax has a list of bipartisan backers. But it’s far from a sure thing.

media:content.media:title: President Donald Trump discussed a fuel tax holiday during an event at the White House on Monday.

item.dc:creator: By Pavan Acharya, Amelia Davidson and Meredith Lee Hill
item.dc:contributor: By Pavan Acharya, Amelia Davidson and Meredith Lee Hill

item.title: Trump picks Cameron Hamilton to run FEMA
item.description: The president nominated the man he fired as leader of the nation's disaster agency a year ago.

media:content.media:title: Cameron Hamilton testifies on Capitol Hill last year before he was fired as acting administrator of FEMA. President Donald Trump nominated him Monday to lead the agency again.

item.dc:creator: By Thomas Frank

item.title: Gas giant met with White House about New England pipeline project
item.description: The plan to increase gas supply comes as the region's Democratic governors seek reelection, pledging to both green their states' economies and address high energy prices.

media:content.media:title: People shovel snow along a residential street in Boston on Jan. 26. New England has long faced concerns about inadequate pipeline capacity during the winter, when gas demand for heating and electricity soars.

item.dc:creator: By Benjamin Storrow

item.title: Gas tax holiday momentum grows with Trump support
item.description: Republicans in both chambers of Congress said they would introduce legislation to suspend the federal gasoline tax.

media:content.media:title: Missouri Sen. Josh Hawley has been a Republican proponent of suspending the federal gasoline tax. On Monday, he announced legislation to that end.

item.dc:creator: By Amelia Davidson and Pavan Acharya
item.dc:contributor: By Amelia Davidson and Pavan Acharya

item.title: U.S. oil rush into Venezuela tests Trump’s democracy promises
item.description: The White House is full steam ahead on growing the oil industry in Venezuela. Elections are another matter.

media:content.media:title: Jarrod Agen, second from left, joins authorities at a ceremony after a U.S. commercial flight landing at Simon Bolivar International Airport in Maiquetia, Venezuela, on April 30, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By James Bikales and Sophia Cai
item.dc:contributor: By James Bikales and Sophia Cai

item.title: ‘Drill Baby Drill’ meets democratic limbo in Venezuela
item.description: The White House is full steam ahead on growing the oil industry in Venezuela. Elections are another matter.

media:content.media:title: Jarrod Agen, second from left, joins authorities at a ceremony after a U.S. commercial flight landing at Simon Bolivar International Airport in Maiquetia, Venezuela, on April 30, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By James Bikales and Sophia Cai
item.dc:contributor: By James Bikales and Sophia Cai

item.title: Chris Wright: ‘I don't know the future of gas prices'
item.description: But the Energy secretary said the U.S. is in a “tremendous position" despite rising gas prices.

media:content.media:title: Energy Secretary Chris Wright speaks during an event with President Donald Trump on May 4, 2026, in the White House.

item.dc:creator: By Cheyanne M. Daniels

item.title: Greens disavowed RFK Jr. Now they’re rethinking his MAHA movement
item.description: Environmental groups publicly rejected Robert F. Kennedy Jr. during his presidential run. Now, some are quietly locking arms with the "Make America Healthy Again" movement the longtime environmental attorney founded.

media:content.media:title: Culture Apothecary podcaster Alex Clark holds a bottle of Roundup weed and grass killer as she speaks at a rally outside the U.S. Supreme Court as justices heard oral arguments in Monsanto Company v. Durnell in Washington on April 27.

item.dc:creator: By Ellie Borst

item.title: A data center drained 30M gallons of water unnoticed — until residents complained about low water pressure
item.description: Residents in Fayetteville, Georgia, noticed low water pressure last year. The utility discovered two unaccounted-for water connections at one of the nation’s largest data center campuses.

media:content.media:title: The Atlanta skyline looms over a field of dry grass during a 2019 drought. Georgia is dealing with similar conditions this year.

item.dc:creator: By Arianna Skibell

item.title: The Northeast is set for a star turn in the affordability debate
item.description: State policymakers appear unprepared for a potential increase in power bills driven by a doubling of emissions credit prices as Virginia gears up to rejoin a regional climate program.

media:content.media:title: Virginia Gov. Abigail Spanberger (D), then a member of Congress, listens during a 2024 interview in Henrico County, Virginia.

item.dc:creator: By Marie J. French, Adam Aton, Ry Rivard and Mona Zhang
item.dc:contributor: By Marie J. French, Adam Aton, Ry Rivard and Mona Zhang

item.title: Trump gets his chance to upend FEMA
item.description: Long-anticipated recommendations to revamp the disaster agency were approved Thursday. Now, President Donald Trump has to make a decision.

media:content.media:title: President Donald Trump and Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin visit the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool on Thursday.

item.dc:creator: By Thomas Frank

item.title: FEMA said it answered the phone during the Texas floods. Most callers didn’t get through.
item.description: The agency’s top official told lawmakers that the “vast majority” of calls were answered. New data indicates most people couldn’t get through.

media:content.media:title: Flood waters leave debris including vehicles and equipment scattered in Louise Hays Park in Kerrville, Texas, on July 5, 2025.

item.dc:creator: By Thomas Frank

item.title: Diesel price spike raises stakes for Trump, GOP ahead of midterms
item.description: A surge in diesel costs is set to ripple through the U.S. economy, raising prices on everything from groceries to housing and adding new political risk for Republicans.

media:content.media:title: A gasoline price board is shown at a gas station, Thursday, March 26, 2026, in Las Vegas.

item.dc:creator: By Scott Waldman and Ben Lefebvre
item.dc:contributor: By Scott Waldman and Ben Lefebvre

item.title: The federal Oscars are having trouble finding candidates
item.description: "Disruption and upheaval" during the Trump administration led to fewer nominations — and fewer winners — of awards considered the federal government’s Oscars.

media:content.media:title: The WWII Memorial, Washington Monument, U.S. Capitol and National Mall are seen from the air on Aug. 23, 2025, in Washington.

item.dc:creator: By Kevin Bogardus

item.title: Musk promised his data center would reuse water. That's now stalled.
item.description: Elon Musk’s artificial intelligence company abruptly stopped work on a water reuse facility meant to alleviate strain on the Memphis-area water supply. Nobody’s saying why.

media:content.media:credit: Steve Jones, Flight by Southwings for Southern Environmental Law Center
media:content.media:title: An aerial view from April 2025 of the xAI data center, known as Colossus, in Memphis, Tennessee.

item.dc:creator: By Ariel Wittenberg and Miranda Willson
item.dc:contributor: By Ariel Wittenberg and Miranda Willson

item.title: Gallego’s energy plan: Pull the Democrats to the center
item.description: The Arizona senator wants his party to focus on energy affordability over climate change appeals to win control in November.

media:content.media:title: Sen. Ruben Gallego (D-Ariz.) speaks at the People's State Of The Union Rally And Boycott Outside The Capitol on the National Mall, Feb. 24, 2026, in Washington.

item.dc:creator: By Zack Colman

item.title: Data centers used to be a prize. States are having second thoughts.
item.description: Legislators in at least 28 states this year introduced bills that would roll back tax incentives for the energy-hungry facilities.

media:content.media:title: Dale Browne, president of the Great Oak Homeowners Association, speaks at a 2022 rally near Manassas, Virginia, in opposition to a newly built data center for Amazon Web Services.

item.dc:creator: By Jason Plautz and Jeffrey Tomich
item.dc:contributor: By Jason Plautz and Jeffrey Tomich

item.title: Hard choices test breakaway climate summit
item.description: A first-of-its-kind conference that focused on phasing out fossil fuels now confronts the challenge of turning plans into policy.

media:content.media:title: Colombia's Environment Minister Irene Velez (left) and the Netherlands' Climate and Green Growth Minister Stientje van Veldhoven oversaw the International Conference on the Just Transition Away from Fossil Fuels in Santa Marta, Colombia.

item.dc:creator: By Sara Schonhardt

item.title: Trump bets on quick Iran oil crunch. Experts see prolonged pain and rising costs.
item.description: Administration officials say Tehran is days from crisis, but analysts see a slower squeeze with global price shocks already hitting U.S. consumers and reshaping the political fight.

media:content.media:title: A gas station on the 5000 block of Connecticut Ave NW in Washington as Americans continue to feel the squeeze from rising fuel costs driven by global oil disruptions linked to the Iran war.

item.dc:creator: By Scott Waldman

item.title: Lawmakers agree the data center boom shouldn’t hurt ratepayers. Now comes the hard part.
item.description: Democrats and Republicans concur on shielding people from energy costs associated with data centers. That doesn’t mean they’re close to passing a law.

media:content.media:title: House Energy and Commerce Chair Brett Guthrie (R-Ky.) said he would like to see the "Ratepayer Protection Act" reach the floor.

item.dc:creator: By Nico Portuondo

item.title: US mounts new bid to block shipping carbon tax
item.description: The Trump administration has been circulating flyers at this week’s gathering of the International Maritime Organization.

media:content.media:title: A Maersk line container ship approaches PortMiami on Sept. 30, 2022, in Miami Beach, Florida.

item.dc:creator: By Sara Schonhardt, Martina Sapio and Charlie Cooper
item.dc:contributor: By Sara Schonhardt, Martina Sapio and Charlie Cooper

item.title: White House officials leading new delegation to Venezuela for energy, mining agreements
item.description: The Trump administration has overseen efforts to drive new investment in the country's oil and mining sectors to help ease bottlenecks in the commodities.

media:content.media:credit: John Lamparski/AFP via Getty Images for Concordia Annual Summit
media:content.media:title: Jarrod Agen, executive director of the National Energy Dominance Council, speaks onstage on Sept. 23, 2025, in New York City.

item.dc:creator: By Ben Lefebvre and Hannah Northey
item.dc:contributor: By Ben Lefebvre and Hannah Northey

item.title: White House meets with Chevron, oil traders amid Iran standoff
item.description: The latest meeting comes as gasoline prices reach new 4-year highs as supplies shrink.

media:content.media:title: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent testifies before the Senate Appropriations Subcommittee on Financial Services and General Government on Capitol Hill on April 22, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Ben Lefebvre

item.title: Trump’s reflecting pool spruce-up fails to charm preservationists
item.description: The president ordered the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool to be coated with a blue liner.

media:content.media:title: Work is being done on the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool on Tuesday in Washington.

item.dc:creator: By Heather Richards and Rylan DiGiacomo-Rapp
item.dc:contributor: By Heather Richards and Rylan DiGiacomo-Rapp

item.title: ‘There’s a day of reckoning coming’: Energy experts expect another spike at the pump
item.description: Trump's jawboning of the markets is sending the wrong signals to oil producers by keeping crude prices artificially low.

media:content.media:title: Gas prices are displayed at a station in Brooklyn, New York, on April 21, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Scott Waldman and Eli Stokols
item.dc:contributor: By Scott Waldman and Eli Stokols

item.title: Zeldin on firm ground as Cabinet ousters continue
item.description: There might just be a simple reason the EPA administrator is more secure in his job than his Cabinet colleagues: He’s good at doing what Trump wants.

media:content.media:title: President Donald Trump and EPA Administrator Lee Zeldin announced a rollback of the 2009 endangerment finding in the White House's Roosevelt Room on Feb. 12.

item.dc:creator: By Alex Guillén, Jean Chemnick and Kevin Bogardus
item.dc:contributor: By Alex Guillén, Jean Chemnick and Kevin Bogardus

item.title: The world is searching for oil. This summit is looking to get rid of it.
item.description: The gathering in Colombia marks a breakaway effort to accelerate climate action after years of plodding progress under the United Nations.

media:content.media:title: Colombia's environmental minister, Irene Vélez Torres, will help lead a climate conference that stems from frustration with U.N. delays.

item.dc:creator: By Sara Schonhardt

item.title: US official: No hope for global carbon tax
item.description: A U.S. negotiator warned other countries in a closed-door meeting that efforts to rein in climate pollution from ships has "no prospect" of success.

media:content.media:title: A container ship approaches the port of Santos, Brazil, on April 1, 2025.

item.dc:creator: By Sara Schonhardt

item.title: No quick rebound from Hormuz disruption, oil execs tell Dallas Fed
item.description: A Federal Reserve Bank of Dallas survey captured the oil and gas industry’s rising frustration with the Trump administration over the uncertainty and increased shipping costs fomented by the war in Iran.

media:content.media:title: Anonymous responses to the survey from 116 executives in the heart of the U.S. energy industry captured the sector’s rising frustration with the Trump administration over the uncertainty and increased shipping costs fomented by the conflict.

item.dc:creator: By James Bikales

item.title: NRC chair dismisses claims of Trump interference
item.description: Democrats pointed to staffing cuts and new reactor licensing pathways as potential threats to the Nuclear Regulatory Commission's independence.

media:content.media:title: Nuclear Regulatory Commission Chair Ho Nieh on Capitol Hill on Wednesday.

item.dc:creator: By Nico Portuondo
